items.title: An AI bubble may be here, but the bubble is bigger for 'fad trades'
items.description: While artificial intelligence dominated market themes in 2025, all Wall Street can talk about now is whether the market (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) is already in or about to enter an AI bubble. Annandale Capital founder and chairman George Seay and CFRA Research chief investment strategist Sam Stovall share their thoughts on whether an AI bubble is forming or already here, and the areas displaying the most exuberance. Also catch Sam Stovall discuss Wall Street's Santa Claus rally optimism and George Seay's comments on small-cap stocks in 2026. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Opening Bid.

items.title: S&P 493 likely to see 'outsized returns' in 2026, outside Mag 7
items.description: While the S&P 500 (^GSPC) was largely driven by the Magnificent Seven tech stocks in 2025, how did the rest of the S&P 493 perform and what should Wall Street expect in 2026? Rainwater Equity founder and portfolio manager Joseph Shaposhnik comes on Market Domination to discuss his outlook on the so-called Magnificent 493 and how AI has impacted their growth. The US stock market will close early at 1:00 P.M. today (Christmas Eve) and will be closed all day Thursday for the Christmas holiday. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination.

items.title: Weak Macro Can't Stop E-commerce Stocks Expedia and Amazon
items.description: Macro uncertainties are crimping holiday sales but Expedia and Amazon shareholders have reason to cheer.

items.title: AI & Security Push Aids Google Cloud: What's Ahead for GOOGL Stock?
items.description: Alphabet is leaning on fast-growing Google Cloud, with a $155 billion backlog, as AI, security and capex fuel momentum.

items.title: How Mill closed the deal with Amazon and Whole Foods
items.description: Matt Rogers' food waste startup will soon deploy its commercial food waste bins in grocery stores throughout the U.S. Here's how Rogers thinks about preparing a startup for success.
